content: Laravel's RefreshDatabase trait behaves differently by driver: against SQLite in-memory it wraps everything in a single transaction; against MySQL/PostgreSQL it runs real migrations once per test-class and wraps each individual test in a DB transaction that is rolled back afterward, resetting row data but NOT auto-increment counters — MySQL auto-increment IDs keep climbing across tests/re-seeds within a run. Implication for this phase: an idempotency seeder test must assert on counts/attributes (e.g. User::count(), a firstOrFail() by email), never on a hardcoded primary-key id, since re-seeding within the same test process will not reset MySQL's auto_increment.
